decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the board through removal spells like Cast Down and Defile, while also utilizing cards like Nihil Spellbomb to disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies. The deck also aims to generate value through sacrificing tokens created by cards like Khalni Garden and Ichor Wellspring. Additionally, the deck includes powerful late-game threats like Crypt Rats and Troll of Khazad-dûm to close out the game. The mana base is designed to support the Golgari/Jund color combination and includes utility lands like Bojuka Bog and Witch's Cottage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using a combination of burn spells and aggressive creatures. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace and pressure the opponent early on, using cards like Fiery Temper, Lightning Bolt, and Fireblast to remove blockers and deal direct damage. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery to generate additional value and card advantage. Overall, the deck is designed to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main focus of this Gruul Aggro deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Wild Growth, Utopia Sprawl, and Arbor Elf to get big creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Boarding Party onto the battlefield early. Malevolent Rumble and Ram Through help clear the way for your creatures to deal damage to your opponent, while Avenging Hunter and Bannerhide Krushok provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and removal spells to secure a fast victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent through a combination of direct burn spells and aggressive creatures. By utilizing efficient spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, along with creatures like Guttersnipe and Voldaren Epicure, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they can react. Additionally, cards like Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery provide additional ways to deal damage and maintain board presence. The deck's main strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault.

This deck focuses on utilizing control spells and card draw to shut down opponent's strategies while efficiently playing threats such as Gurmag Angler and Cryptic Serpent. The key strategy involves disrupting opponent's plays with counterspells and removal like Snuff Out, while recycling cards with Thought Scour and Mental Note to maintain card advantage. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by outdrawing them and playing cost-effective threats to close out the game. Additionally, it uses cards like Contaminated Aquifer and Lórien Revealed for mana fixing and card selection.

The primary focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control your opponent's strategy through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By constantly interacting with the opponent's spells and creatures, you can establish a dominant position on the battlefield while also using card advantage to maintain your resources. The deck also includes some finishers like Gurmag Angler and Thorn of the Black Rose to close out the game once you have gained control. Overall, the goal is to outlast and outmaneuver your opponent until you can secure the win.

The main focus of this Pauper 1 vs 1 Aggro - Elves deck is to establish a quick and aggressive board presence using low-cost Elf creatures. By utilizing cards like Avenging Hunter, Quirion Ranger, and Generous Ent, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures and pump them up with cards like Timberwatch Elf. The goal is to keep up the pressure and use cards like Lead the Stampede to refill your hand and maintain momentum. The deck is heavily focused on accelerating mana production with cards like Priest of Titania to power out threats quickly.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifact creatures and generating value through sacrificing artifacts. By combining aggressive creatures with card draw and direct damage spells, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient board presence. Utilizing various artifact lands and mana sources, the deck can consistently cast its spells and maintain a steady flow of threats. The addition of sacrifice outlets like Makeshift Munitions and Nihil Spellbomb allows for additional flexibility and potential removal options, while synergizing with the artifact theme of the deck.

The deck focuses on using Gates to control the board and disrupt opponents, while also utilizing card advantage and protection spells to maintain a strong position. Prismatic Strands and counterspells help protect key threats, while cards like Journey to Nowhere and Outlaw Medic provide removal options. Guardian of the Guildpact serves as a solid threat, while Squadron Hawk and Brainstorm help filter through the deck for consistency. The deck aims to outvalue opponents with efficient spells and resilient creatures, while taking advantage of the synergy between Gates and control elements.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap artifacts and creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a high number of threats. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Myr Enforcer, the deck aims to put constant pressure on the opponent while also efficiently drawing cards with Ichor Wellspring and Thoughtcast. The inclusion of cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Krark-Clan Shaman also allows for disruption of the opponent's graveyard and removal of threats. With a combination of aggressive creatures and efficient card draw, the deck aims to win through fast, relentless aggression.

The main concept of this Aggro-Affinity deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of cheap artifacts and efficient creatures. By utilizing artifact lands and cards that benefit from artifacts being on the battlefield, such as Myr Enforcer and Refurbished Familiar, the deck aims to quickly flood the board with threats while utilizing cards like Thoughtcast to maintain card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Krark-Clan Shaman and Galvanic Blast also provide removal options and direct damage to help secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a swarm of Elf creatures, utilizing cards like Quirion Ranger, Lead the Stampede, and Generous Ent to generate a strong board presence. The goal is to quickly build up an army of Elves and buff them with cards like Timberwatch Elf to deal massive damage to the opponent. With a strong emphasis on ramping up mana with cards like Priest of Titania and Fyndhorn Elves, this deck aims to utilize its efficient creatures and spells to win the game in the early turns.

The main focus of this deck revolves around disrupting and controlling the opponent's strategy through a combination of card draw, discard, counterspells, and removal spells. By utilizing cards that allow for efficient filtering and manipulation of the deck, along with powerful creatures and instant-speed interaction, the Dimir Control deck seeks to outvalue and outlast its opponent in a game of attrition. With a strong emphasis on hand disruption and board control, this deck aims to limit the opponent's resources while gradually building its own advantage to secure a victory.

This deck utilizes the Balustrade Spy combo to quickly mill your deck and put creatures into your graveyard. By utilizing cards like Generous Ent and Saruli Caretaker to ramp up your mana, you can easily bring back a Lotleth Giant or Troll of Khazad-dûm for a big finish. Additionally, cards like Masked Vandal and Mesmeric Fiend provide disruption and removal options. With a focus on fast mana generation and creature searches, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with a powerful combo finish.

This deck focuses on quickly equipping creatures with powerful auras to pump them up and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. The key strategy is to play low-cost creatures like Slippery Bogle and Gladecover Scout, then attach powerful auras like Ethereal Armor and Armadillo Cloak to boost their power and toughness. The deck also includes mana ramp cards like Utopia Sprawl and Abundant Growth to help quickly cast spells and maintain a strong board presence. By combining efficient creatures with powerful auras, this deck aims to quickly take control of the game and beat down the opponent.

This Jund aggro deck focuses on efficient removal spells and creatures that can generate value through sacrificing artifacts and lands. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar and Writhing Chrysalis, the deck can quickly build a threatening board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal spells like Cast Down and Nihil Spellbomb. This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and resource denial, generating incremental advantage through sacrificing artifacts and lands for value.
